steps.title: Locate the median positions
steps.content: Data set A has $50$ values, so its median uses the $25$th and $26$th values. After adding $18$, data set B has $51$ values, so its median is the $26$th value.

steps.title: Test the median claim
steps.content: The added value $18$ is less than every value shown in the histogram, so it becomes the first value in data set B. The $26$th value of B is the old $25$th value of A. Since the old $25$th and $26$th values could be equal, the median is not guaranteed to decrease.

steps.title: Test the mean claim
steps.content: Every value in data set A is greater than $18$, so the mean of A is greater than $18$. Adding a value below the current mean must decrease the mean.

steps.title: Combine the statements
steps.content: Statement I is not guaranteed, but statement II must be true. The answer is B.
